strengths: Current hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq directly aligns with the agentic AI architecture mandate
Distributed, low-latency production experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second demonstrates the backend and event-streaming depth required
Fintech exposure at RIDE Capital (German investment platform) combined with software craftsmanship discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — supports regulated, high-stakes shipping
concerns: No explicit CTO or VP-level title; senior IC track record rather than documented experience scaling teams from 10 to 30+
Multi-agent production systems experience is recent (2025–present) rather than spanning multiple years at scale
Limited evidence of board-level communication or governance of outsourced engineering partners

key_factors.factor: Hands-on agentic AI and MCP framework work at re:cinq
High-throughput distributed systems and event-driven architecture depth
Fintech platform experience at RIDE Capital with craftsmanship rigor

response.justification: The candidate combines an unusual trifecta directly relevant to this role: active, hands-on construction of agentic AI infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq; proven distributed-systems chops from operating microservices at millions of requests per second at Sociomantic; and regulated fintech exposure at RIDE Capital paired with disciplined engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development). Founding and operating GISCollective as an event-driven, Kubernetes-deployable platform shows architectural ownership end-to-end, and a long history of teaching, workshops, and open-source leadership signals the communication and mentorship capacity needed to grow a senior technical layer. The profile reads as a builder-leader well-positioned to architect the modular, observable, model-agnostic agent stack this CTO mandate demands.
